q: The bond dissociation energy is highest for
question: The bond dissociation energy is highest for
solution:
Fluorine (F2), Chlorine Cl2, Bromine Br2, Iodine I2 are called halogen compounds, they occur as diatomic molecules in which a covalent bond is formed after sharing 1 electron with each other. As it moves down from chlorine to iodine in the halogen group, the atomic size increases, and hence bond length also increases.

As bond length increases, the energy required to break bonds (dissociation energy) decreases.

So, chlorine will have the highest bond energy.

q: Correct statement about smog is
question: Correct statement about smog is
options: Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidizing agents
Classical smog also has high concentration of oxidizing agents
solution:
A. Classical smog occurs in cool humid climate. It is a mixture of smoke, fog, and sulphur dioxide. Chemically it is a reducing mixture and so it is called reducing smog. So statement A is incorrect.

B. Classical smog is a mixture of smoke, fog and SO2. NO2 is not present in classical smog. So, statement B is incorrect.

C. Photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate. The main components of the photochemical smog result from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydrocarbons and nitrogen oxides produced by automobiles and factories. Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidising agents and is, therefore, called as oxidising smog. So, statement C is correct.

D. Classical smog is a reducing mixture, so it is called as reducing smog. Hence, statement D is incorrect.

q: The reaction representing the Mond process for metal refining is ______
question: The reaction representing the Mond process for metal refining is ______
solution:
Mond process for metal refining: In this process, nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl:

Ni + 4CO →330 - 350K Ni(CO)4

The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal:

Ni(CO)4 →450 - 470K Ni + 4CO

question: “<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>” obtained by Ostwald's method involving air oxidation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>, upon further air oxidation produces “<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>”. “<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>” on hydration forms an oxoacid of Nitrogen along with evolution of “<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>”. The oxoacid also produces “<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>” and gives positive brown ring test
solution:
Ostwald's method is based upon catalytic oxidation of NH3 by atmospheric oxygen.

4NH3 (g) +5O2 (g) →500K, 9 barPt/Rh gauge catalyst 4NO (g) (A)+6H2O (g)

Nitric oxide thus formed combines with oxygen giving NO2.

2NO (g) +O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g)(B)

Nitrogen dioxide so formed, dissolves in water to give HNO3.

3NO2 (g) + H2O (l) → 2HNO3 (aq) + NO (g)

∴ A and B are NO and NO2 respectively.

q: The magnetic behaviour of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Li</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, respectively, are
question: The magnetic behaviour of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Li</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, respectively, are
solution:
In the structure Li2O each lithium atom loses one electron to form 2 cations Li+ and oxygen atom gains two electrons to form the anion O2-.  It has no unpaired electrons. So, it is diamagnetic in nature. 

The peroxide O22- present in  Na2O2 has 4 electron in π*2p, thus all are paired. So, it is diamagnetic in nature.

In the structure KO2 oxygen is present in O2-. The number of electrons present is 17, where the where the sixteen electrons are from two oxygen atoms and one from the negative charge. There is one unpaired electron present in the last orbital. Hence, it is paramagnetic in nature. 

Li2O→ diamagnetic

Na2O2→ diamagnetic

KO2→ paramagnetic

question:
The correct order of hydration enthalpies is

(A) K+
(B) Rb+
(C) Mg2+
(D) Cs+
(E) Ca2+

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
The hydration enthalpy of alkali metal ions decreases with an increase in ionic sizes.

So, the order is: Li+ > Na+ >  K+ > Rb+ > Cs+

Like alkali metal ions, the hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metal ions decrease with an increase in ionic size down the group.

So, the order is: Be2+ > Mg2+ > Ca2+ > Sr2+ > Ba2+ 

The hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metal ions are larger than those of alkali metal ions. 

So, the correct order is: Mg2+ > Ca2+ > K+ > Rb+ > Cs+

Given : Kb=0.5K kg mol-1 and Kf=1.8kg mol-1. Assume molality to be equal to molarity in all cases.

What is the value of compressibility factor at point A?
question:
For 1 mol of gas, the plot of pV vs p is shown below. p is the pressure and V is the volume of the gas.

What is the value of compressibility factor at point A?
solution:
According to Van der Waals equation,

(P + aVm2) (Vm - b) = RT

Where, Vm = molar volume of the gas

R = Universal gas constant

T = Temperature

P = Pressure

At point A 'a' is considerable and 'b' is negligible.

So, (P + aVm2) Vm = RT

PVm + aVm = RT

Compressibility factor (Z) = 1 - aVmRT

Hence, the correct answer is 1 - aRTV

q: Which of the following salt solutions would coagulate the colloid soloution formed when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is added to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution, at the fastest rate?
question: Which of the following salt solutions would coagulate the colloid soloution formed when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is added to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution, at the fastest rate?
solution:
Generally, the greater the valence of the flocculating ion added, the greater its power to cause precipitation. In the coagulation of a negative sol, the flocculating power is in the order: 

Al3+ > Ba2+ > Na+

FeCl3 with NaOH forms a negative sol.

So, AlCl3 coagulate it most.

solution:
We know,

 Keq=KfKb

∴ Keq=103102=10
Using gibbs free energy equation,

∴  ΔG°=-RTlnKeq  ⇒ΔG=-RTln10

⇒-8.3×300×2.3=-5.7 kJ mol-1≈6 kJ mole-1 (nearest integer)

Answer =6.

q: Let the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup></math> be in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>8</mn></math>. Then the coefficient of the term, which is in the middle of these three terms, is
question: Let the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup></math> be in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>8</mn></math>. Then the coefficient of the term, which is in the middle of these three terms, is
solution:
Given,

The coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of (1+2x)n be in the ratio 2: 5: 8.

Now rth term in expansion of (1+2x)n is given by, tr+1=Crn(2x)r

Now let Tr, Tr+1, Tr+2 are in the ratio 2:5:8

⇒TrTr+1=Cr-1n(2)r-1Crn(2)r=25

⇒n!(r-1)!(n-r+1)!n!(2)r!(n-r)!=25

⇒rn-r+1=45⇒5r=4n-4r+4 

⇒9r=4(n+1) .......1

Now taking other ratio Tr+1Tr+2=Crn(2)rCr+1n(2)r+1=58

⇒n!r!(n-r)!n!(r+1)!(n-r-1)!=54⇒r+1n-r=54

⇒4r+4=5n-5r

⇒5n-4=9r .........2

From equation 1 & 2 we get,

4n+4=5n-4⇒n=8 and r=4

So, coefficient of middle term will be

C4824=16×8×7×6×54×3×2×1=16×70=1120

solution:
Given,

fx+y=fx+fy & f1=15

Now solving the given functional equation by taking x=1 & y=1 we get,

f1+1=f1+f1

Now taking x=2 & y=1 we get,

question:
Consider the following system of questions

αx+2y+z=1

2αx+3y+z=1

3x+αy+2z=β

For some α,β∈ℝ. Then which of the following is NOT correct.
options: It has no solution for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℝ</mi></math>

It has no solution for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> and for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>2</mn></math>

It has a solution for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math>
solution:
Given,

System of questions

αx+2y+z=1

2αx+3y+z=1

3x+αy+2z=β

Now finding, D=α212α313α2=0

⇒α(6-α)-2(4α-3)+1(2α2-9)=0

⇒α-3(α+1)=0

⇒α=-1,3

Dx=211311α2β=0⇒β=2

⇒2β-2-1(3β-α)+(6-α)=0

⇒β=2

Dy=α112α1132β=0

⇒α(β-2)-1(2αβ-3)+1(4α-3)=0

⇒α(2-β)=0

⇒α=0,β=2

Dz=α212α313αβ=0

⇒α2-αβ-3=0

Now when β=2,α=-1 the system of equations will have infinite solutions,

When α=3, β=2 the system has infinite solution,

And when α=3, β≠2 the system has no solution as Dx & Dy≠0 for β≠2,

Hence, only option B is not correct

solution:
Given,

y(x+1)dx-x2dy=0

⇒x+1x2dx=dyy

⇒1x+1x2dx=dyy

Now integrating both side we get,

logex-1x=logey+c

Now on using y1=e we get, c=-2

So, the equation of curve becomes logex-1x=logey-2

⇒y=elnx-1x+2

Hence, limx→0+elnx-1-1x+2=e-∞=0.

solution:
Given,

fx=x2+2x+1x2+1

f'(x)=x2+1(2x+2)-(x2+2x+1)2xx2+12

⇒f'(x)=2x3+2x2+2x+2-2x3-4x2-2xx2+12

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℝ</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℝ</mi></math> be a differentiable function that satisfies the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>∀</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℝ</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>|</mo></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

Functional equation fx+y=fx+fy-1

Now taking x=0 & y=0 in above equation we get,

f0+0=f0+f0-1⇒f0=1

Now we know that,

f'x=limh→0fx+h-fxh

⇒f'x=limh→0fx+fh-1-fxh

⇒f'x=limh→0fh-1h

Now let limh→0fh-1h=k

So, the equation becomes f'x=k

Now putting x=0 in above equation we get,

f'0=k⇒k=2 as given f'0=2

So, f'x=2

Now integrate both side we get,

fx=2x+c

Now again taking x=0 we get,

f0=2×0+c⇒c=1 as f0=1

So, fx=2x+1

And f-2=2×-2+1⇒f-2=-3

Hence, f-2=3

question: Fifteen football players of a club-team are given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> T-shirts with their names written on the backside. If the players pick up the T-shirts randomly, then the probability that at least <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> players pick the correct T-shirt is
solution:
Given,

Fifteen football players of a club-team are given 15 T-shirts with their names written on the backside

So, total sample space will be 15!

Now for at least 3 players pick the correct T-shirt, 

Required probability=1-D15+C115·D14+C215D1315!

Where Dn is de-arrangement and Dn≈n!e where e≈2.73

Taking D15 as 15 ! e, D(14) as 14!e and D(13) as 13!e

We get , 1-15!e+15·14!e+15×142×13!e15!

=1-1e+1e+12e=1-52e≈.08

Note- This question was bonus as NTA as calculated the answer as C315×12!15!=16, they have not considered the repetation cases.

Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>α</mi></mrow><mi>β</mi></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>α</mi></mrow><mi>γ</mi></mfrac></math> are the roots of the equation :
solution:
Given α and β are roots of the below equation

14x2-31x+3λ=0

So, α+β=3114      …1

And, αβ=3λ14       …2

Given α and γ are roots of the below equation

35x2-53x+4λ=0

So, α+γ=5335         …3

And, αγ=4λ35        …4

On dividing equation2  and equation 4,

βγ=3×354×14=158⇒β=158γ

On subtracting equation 1 and equation 3,

⇒β-γ=3114-5335=155-10670=710

⇒158γ-γ=710⇒γ=45

⇒β=158×45=32

So, α=3114-β=3114-32=57

And λ=143αβ=143×57×32=5

So, the sum of the roots of the required equation,

3αβ+4αγ=3αγ+4αββγ

=3×4λ35+4×3λ14βγ=12λ14+3514×35βγ

=49×12×5490×32×45=5

And the product of the roots of the required equation,
=3αβ×4αγ=12α2βγ=12×254932×45=25049

So, the required equation is

x2-(sum of roots)x+product of roots=0

⇒x2-5x+25049=0

⇒ 49x2-245x+250=0

solution:
Given,

a1,a2,a3,…. be a GP of increasing positive numbers,

And the product of fourth and sixth terms is 9

So, a4·a6=9⇒a52=9⇒a5=3

Also given the sum of fifth and seventh terms is 24,

So, a5+a7=24

⇒a5+a5r2=24

⇒1+r2=8⇒r=7

Now using a5=3⇒a1r4=3⇒a1=3r4=349

Then, a1a9+a2a4a9+a5+a7

=349×349×78+349×7×349×73×349×78+349×74+349×76

=9+27+3+21=60

Given,

a→,b→ and c→ be three non-zero non-coplanar vectors,

Also the position vectors of four points A, B, C and D be a→-b→+c→,  λa→-3b→+4c→, -a→+2b→-3c→ and 2a→-4b→+6c→ respectively,

question:
If the height of transmitting and receiving antennas are 80 m each, the maximum line of sight distance will be :

Given : Earth’s radius =6.4×106 m.
solution:
Given: R=6.4×106 m

Using the relation between the range of the radio communication and the height of the transmission antenna hT and the receiving antenna hR, the maximum line of sight distance between two antennas, is dmax=2RhT+2RhR

⇒dmax=2×2×6.4×106×80=64000 m=64 km

question: Ratio of thermal energy released in two resistor <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>R</mi></math> connected in parallel in an electric circuit is :
solution:
In parallel connection, potential difference across resistors remains the same.

Thermal energy released in resistor is given by H=V2R×t

So, the ratio is H1H2=V2tRV2t3R=3RR=3:1.

C. 75 W ultra-violet lamp

D. 10 W ultra-violet lamp

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
The threshold wavelength for photoelectric emission from a material is 5500 A∘. Photoelectrons will be emitted, when this material is illuminated with monochromatic radiation from a

A. 75 W infra-red lamp

B. 10 W infra-red lamp

C. 75 W ultra-violet lamp

D. 10 W ultra-violet lamp

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Given here, threshold wavelength λth=5500 Å=550 nm

For photoelectric emission, the wavelength of the light should be less than 550 nm.

Wavelength of infrared radiation extends from 700 nm-1 mm and that of ultraviolet radiation is 100-400 nm.

Since, UV light has less wavelength than 550 nm, so only UV would be able to emit photoelectrons.

question: Find the mutual inductance in the arrangement, when a small circular loop of wire of radius R is placed inside a large square loop of wire of side L(L≫R). The loops are coplanar and their centres coincide :
solution:
The magnetic field due to the rectangular loop at its centre is:

B=4×μ0i4πL212+12=22μ0iπL

Therefore, the flux through the circular loop ϕ=πR2×B=22μ0R2iL

If M is the mutual inductance, then

Mdidt=dϕdt=22μ0R2Ldidt

⇒M=22μ0R2L

According to the Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, the emf induced in a conducting loop is given by, e=-dϕdt, here, t is time and ϕ=BA is magnetic flux.

=Bπ2rdrdt=2×π×0.1×0.8×2×10-2

 ≈10 mV (rounded off)

Which of the following are true?

A. Speed of light in vacuum is dependent on the direction of propagation.

B. Speed of light in a medium is independent of the wavelength of light.

C. The speed of light is independent of the motion of the source.

D. The speed of light in a medium is independent of intensity.

Choose the correct answer from the question given below :
question:
Which of the following are true?

A. Speed of light in vacuum is dependent on the direction of propagation.

B. Speed of light in a medium is independent of the wavelength of light.

C. The speed of light is independent of the motion of the source.

D. The speed of light in a medium is independent of intensity.

Choose the correct answer from the question given below :
solution:
Speed of light in vaccum is independent of the frequency, wavelength, nature of the source, direction of propagation, motion of the source and intensity of the wave. But it depends on the refractive index of the medium.

Therefore, the speed of light in vacuum is a universal constant.

Hence, C and D are only correct.

Another point charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> is placed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. Charge of proton is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math>. The proton is placed on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis so that the electrostatic force on the proton in zero. In this situation, the position of the proton from the origin is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>.
solution:
Let at point P net electric field is zero.

As direction of electric field due to both charges at point P is opposite, equating magnitude we get

14πε04q0x2=14πε0q0x-122⇒2x-12=x⇒x=24 cm

Distance of proton from origin =24 cm.

solution:
Case 1: No shunt is added

The initial balancing length l of the bridge is,

23=l100-l

⇒200-2l=3l⇒l=40 cm

Case 2: When shunt is added

The new balancing length is, l1=22.5+40=62.5 cm

So, 62.537.5=23X3+X

⇒X=2.

q: Two particles of equal mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> move in a circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> under the action of their mutual gravitational attraction. The speed of each particle will be :
question: Two particles of equal mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> move in a circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> under the action of their mutual gravitational attraction. The speed of each particle will be :
solution:
Let speed of both the particles be v.

Then, gravitational force between the particles will provide the required centripetal force.

Therefore,

Gmm2r2=mv2r

⇒v=Gm4r

[Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>]
question: A car is moving on a horizontal curved road with radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. The approximate maximum speed of car will be, if friction between tyres and road is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>34</mn></math>. [Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>]
solution:
For a car moving with uniform speed along a circular track, the centripetal acceleration is provided by the frictional force acting radially inwards. Therefore, applying Newton's second law along radial direction,

μmg=mv2r

⇒v=μrg

   =0.34×50×10

    ≈13 m s-1

q: A block of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> slides down the plane inclined at angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with an acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>g</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> . The value of coefficient of kinetic friction will be :
question: A block of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> slides down the plane inclined at angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with an acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>g</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> . The value of coefficient of kinetic friction will be :
solution:
Applying Newton's second law along the direction perpendicular to inlined plane:

N-mgcos30∘=0 (As θ=30°)

(since the acceleration of block in the direction perpendicular to the inclined plane is 0)

∴N=mgcos30∘   .....(1)

Let μ be the coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the inclined plane. Applying Newton's second law along inclined plane:

mgsin30∘-μN=ma=mg4    ....(2)

(since the acceleration along the inclined plane,  a=g4)

∴mgsin30∘-μmgcos30∘=mg4   (from equation 1 and 2)

⇒mg2-32μmg=mg4

⇒μ=123
